title: Boundary equivalence for multi-inputs
summary: Lifts unary equality modulo Q and its weighted refinement pointwise to every slot of a rank-r ambient input tuple.

mathematicalContent.heading: Pointwise boundary agreement
mathematicalContent.body: Two r-tuples F and G represent the same tuple of boundary data when every difference Fᵢ−Gᵢ vanishes modulo Q.

mathematicalContent.heading: Weighted boundary agreement
mathematicalContent.body: At multiweight w, each slot uses the unary weighted relation: Fᵢ−Gᵢ=Qhᵢ with hᵢ carrying weight wᵢ−2.

mathematicalContent.heading: Equivalence properties
mathematicalContent.body: The unweighted pointwise relation is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. Weighted boundary agreement forgets to unweighted agreement slot by slot.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines equality of boundary values componentwise for rank-r tuples.
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValues {r : ℕ} (F G : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines weighted boundary equivalence componentwise using the weight wᵢ in each slot.
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValueAtWeights {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (F G : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves componentwise reflexivity of unweighted boundary equivalence.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValues_refl {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues F F

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves componentwise symmetry of unweighted boundary equivalence.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValues_symm {r : ℕ} {F G : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hFG : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues F G) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues G F

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves componentwise transitivity of unweighted boundary equivalence.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValues_trans {r : ℕ} {F G K : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hFG : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues F G) (hGK : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues G K) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues F K

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forgets the homogeneity information in the weighted relation and retains equality of boundary values in every slot.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValueAtWeights.sameBoundaryValues {r : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} {F G : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hFG : CalConf.SameBoundaryValueAtWeights w F G) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValues F G

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The proofs lift the unary modulo-Q relations from Ambienttangential.lean. This file does not prove that arbitrary boundary data admit homogeneous ambient extensions, and it does not establish reflexivity, symmetry, or transitivity for the weighted relation itself.
